extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia timed his sprint perfectly in final 200m after BUD van Poppel's leadout disintegrated and BIASOLETTO's 900m suicide attack failed
tactical_patterns: BIASOLETTO attacked at 900m remaining, forcing teams to chase early and disrupting organized sprint trains before fading at 400m.
Lotto-Jumbo committed at 1500m out but lost VAN POPPEL in finale, revealing vulnerability when leadout initiates too early.
EF Education physically blocked road with pink-shouldered riders sub-1500m, showing aggressive positioning to protect MODOLO's position.
VIVIANI positioned himself dead last with 400m remaining, demonstrating patient timing beats early acceleration even in chaotic finishes.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: won from nowhere after being invisible until final 200m, superior timing and acceleration in messy sprint
rider_signals.BIASOLETTO Samuele: neutral: 900m attack disrupted sprint but lacked power to sustain, effective spoiler tactic without personal reward
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: negative: lost sprint despite perfect 1500m Lotto-Jumbo leadout, disappeared when it mattered most
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: completely absent from final despite QuickStep presence, poor positioning or form issue
rider_signals.MODOLO Sacha: neutral: EF Education committed bodies to protect him but lacked final punch to capitalize
rider_signals.CHAVEZ Esteban: negative: abandoned gruppetto 134km from finish to bank time for next day, revealing fragility before mountain stage
